docs.title: pymorsed
docs.text: Python library for encoding, decoding, generating, and analyzing Morse code audio signals.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Text to Morse conversion
  • Morse to Text conversion
  • Multi-language support
  • Morse Audio Signal generation
  • Morse Audio Signal decoding from file
  • Waveform visualization

docs.title: Supported Languages
docs.text:
Language Code English english Russian russian

Example:

from pymorsed.utils import load_language

mapping = load_language("russian")

Tip

Additional language mappings will be added in future releases.

docs.title: License
docs.text: pymorsed is released under the MIT License. See the LICENSE file for full details.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page documents all public functions provided by the pymorsed library.

docs.title: Encoder Module
docs.text: from pymorsed.encoder import encode

docs.title: encode(text, language="english")
docs.text: Converts plain text into Morse code.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text str Text to encode into Morse code language str Language mapping to use ("english", "russian")

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
str

Morse code representation of the input text.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed.encoder import encode

morse = encode("HELLO")
print(morse)

Output:

.... . .-.. .-.. ---

docs.title: Decoder Module
docs.text: from pymorsed.decoder import decode

docs.title: decode(morse, language="english")
docs.text: Converts Morse code back into plain text.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description morse str Morse code string language str Language mapping used during decoding

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
str

Decoded text.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed.decoder import decode

text = decode(".... . .-.. .-.. ---")
print(text)

Output:

HELLO

docs.title: play_audio(audio)
docs.text: Plays a generated Morse code waveform through the system speakers.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type audio numpy.ndarray

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed.audio_encoder import *

audio = morse_to_audio("... --- ...")
play_audio(audio)

docs.title: save_audio(audio, filename, fs=44100)
docs.text: Saves a Morse waveform as a WAV file.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description audio numpy.ndarray Audio waveform filename str Output filename fs int Sample rate

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed.audio_encoder import *

audio = morse_to_audio("... --- ...")
save_audio(audio, "sos.wav")

docs.title: plot_waveform(audio, fs=44100)
docs.text: Displays the waveform using Matplotlib.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type audio numpy.ndarray fs int

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed.audio_encoder import *

audio = morse_to_audio("... --- ...")
plot_waveform(audio)

docs.title: Audio Decoder Module
docs.text: from pymorsed.audio_decoder import decode_from_file

docs.title: decode_from_file(filepath)
docs.text:
Decodes Morse code from a WAV audio file.

The decoder performs:

  1. Audio loading
  2. Stereo-to-mono conversion
  3. Silence trimming
  4. Envelope extraction
  5. Signal smoothing
  6. Thresholding
  7. Morse symbol reconstruction
  8. Text decoding

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description filepath str Path to WAV file

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
str

Decoded text.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed.audio_decoder import decode_from_file

text = decode_from_file("sos.wav")
print(text)

Output:

SOS

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text:
May raise:

FileNotFoundError

If the audio file does not exist.

RuntimeError

If decoding fails due to invalid audio data.

docs.title: Top-Level Imports
docs.text:
The package exposes commonly used functions directly from the root package.

from pymorsed import encode
from pymorsed import decode
from pymorsed import morse_to_audio

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pymorsed import encode, decode

morse = encode("HELLO")
text = decode(morse)

print(morse)
print(text)

Output:

.... . .-.. .-.. ---
HELLO

docs.title: Supported Languages
docs.text:
Current language mappings:

Language Code English english Russian russian

Additional language mappings may be added in future releases.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install pymorsed

docs.title: Requirements:
docs.text: • Python 3.8+

pymorsed/
├── src/
│   └── pymorsed/
│       ├── __init__.py

│       ├── decoder.py
│       ├── audio_encoder.py
│       ├── audio_decoder.py
│       └── languages/
│           ├── english.json
│           └── hindi.json
├── tests/

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
The recommended way is to import directly from the package namespace.

from pymorsed import encode, decode, morse_to_audio

morse = encode("HELLO")
print(morse)

text = decode(morse)
print(text)

docs.title: Morse Code Spacing Rules
docs.text: pymorsed follows standard Morse code spacing conventions when encoding and decoding messages.

docs.title: Letter Separation
docs.text:
Individual Morse symbols within a letter are written together without spaces.

Examples:

Character Morse Code A .- B -... S ... O ---

docs.title: Word Separation
docs.text:
Letters are separated by a single space.

Example:

HELLO

becomes:

.... . .-.. .-.. ---

Notice that each Morse letter is separated by one space.

docs.title: Multiple Words
docs.text:
Words are separated using the forward slash character (/).

Example:

HELLO WORLD

becomes:

.... . .-.. .-.. --- / .-- --- .-. .-.. -..

The slash represents a word boundary.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Morse Pattern Meaning . Dot - Dash Space () Separates letters Slash (/) Separates words

Note

When manually providing Morse code to `decode()`, use a single space between letters and a forward slash (`/`) between words.

docs.title: Import Individual Modules
docs.text: You can also import functionality from specific modules.

docs.title: Text Encoding
docs.text:
from pymorsed.encoder import encode

morse = encode("HELLO")
print(morse)

docs.title: Text Decoding
docs.text:
from pymorsed.decoder import decode

text = decode(".... . .-.. .-.. ---")
print(text)

docs.title: Audio Encoding
docs.text:
from pymorsed.audio_encoder import morse_to_audio

morse_to_audio("... --- ...")

docs.title: Audio Decoding
docs.text:
from pymorsed.audio_decoder import decode_from_file

text = decode_from_file("sos.wav")
print(text)

docs.title: Import the Entire Package
docs.text:
import pymorsed

morse = pymorsed.encode("HELLO")
text = pymorsed.decode(morse)

This approach is useful when working with multiple functions from the library.
